title: How Is Final Expense Insurance Different from Using Savings?
description: Final expense insurance provides certainty, while savings can be depleted unexpectedly.
callout: **Savings can be used up by emergencies, but final expense insurance guarantees funds for your family when they need it most.**

content.text: **It seems wise to save money to use for your final expenses. However, you might have to reach into that money before you pass away. There’s no way to know for sure because diseases, injuries, and illnesses are unpredictable. Also, medical debt is the #1 cause of bankruptcy in this country. A single stretch in the hospital could erase your entire savings. Paying into a final expense insurance policy creates more certainty than depending solely on personal savings.**

variations: Should I just save for my funeral instead of buying insurance?
Is insurance better than using my savings for final expenses?

title: What is Final Expense Insurance?
description: A smaller permanent life insurance policy for funeral and end-of-life costs.
callout: **Final expense is a permanent life insurance policy designed to cover funeral and immediate end-of-life expenses.**

content.text: **Final expense is a smaller permanent life insurance policy typically intended to cover funeral costs and other end-of-life expenses. Guaranteed payout to helps loved ones pay for a funeral service, burial or cremation, doctor or hospital bills, or other immediate expenses after a death.**

variations: What does final expense insurance cover?
Is final expense the same as burial insurance?

title: Is Final Expense Insurance the same as Life Insurance?
description: Final expense is a type of whole life insurance, but with lower coverage amounts and affordable premiums.
callout: **Final expense insurance is a form of whole life insurance sold in small quantities, with affordable premiums.**

content.text: **Final expense insurance is a form of whole life insurance sold in small quantities. Since coverage amounts are lower than other types of life insurance, like term or permanent insurance, the premiums for a final expense policy tend to be affordable. Coverage amounts can range from a few thousand dollars up to $40,000, in some cases.**

variations: Is final expense the same as term life?
How is final expense different from regular life insurance?

title: What is the difference between whole life and term life?
description: Whole life is permanent with cash value; term life is temporary and expires.
callout: **Whole life insurance lasts your entire life and builds cash value. Term life is temporary and expires after a set period.**

content.text: **Typically final expense coverage is a lower death benefit amount; $5,000 – $40,000. Whole Life Insurance benefits can go up to millions of dollars, and it focuses on cash value accumulation. In reality, a final expense whole life is a type of whole life that is focused more on getting you a more affordable, guaranteed death benefit.**

variations: Is term life or whole life better for final expenses?
Does final expense build cash value?

title: What is the difference between whole life and universal life?
description: Whole life has fixed premiums and cash value; universal life is flexible.
callout: **Whole life offers consistent premiums and guaranteed cash value; universal life offers flexibility.**

content.text: **Whole life and universal life insurance are both types of permanent life insurance. Whole life insurance offers consistent premiums and guaranteed cash value accumulation, while a universal policy provides flexible premiums and death benefits. You can borrow against the cash value of a whole or universal policy.**

variations: Should I get whole life or universal life for final expenses?
